title: VerdictEvent
description: The message emitted by the execution side across the Build seam (spec §5.1), to an event stream, when a unit reaches a verdict. This schema is a LOCAL PROJECTION of the normative VerdictEvent contract published on the contracts tier — ai-development-contracts v0.1.0, schemas/verdict-event.schema.json — which GOVERNS. SELF-DESCRIBING: a consumer that never observed the emission can reconcile it from the event alone. The executor emits and is done — it does not wait, retry, or know who listens (the producer holds no knowledge of any consumer). Produced artifacts travel back per the WorkUnit's declared artifact-delivery mode; every artifact is content-hash identified in both modes.

event-id.description: Unique identifier for this emitted event.

emitted-at.description: When the executor emitted the verdict (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601). A verdict emitted while no consumer is listening sits in the stream until one next reads it — temporal decoupling — so the timestamp is the emit time, not a consume time.

unit-ref.description: Echoes the WorkUnit's `unit-ref`, letting a consumer match the verdict to the unit with no shared state.

parent-deliverable.description: Echoes the WorkUnit's `parent-deliverable`, enabling a consumer to roll the verdict up to its deliverable.

bundle-hash.description: The exact frozen bundle this verdict ran against — equal to the WorkUnit's `bundle-hash`. Closes the loop: every verdict is attributable to an immutable input, so provenance is reproducible.

verdict.description: The foundation verdict vocabulary (spec §6.2) — a declared artifact, never a boolean in an exit code. `accepted`: the artifact met its schema and gates. `rejected`: it did not. `escalate`: the executor could not reach an accept/reject at its current tier and recommends a consumer re-dispatch one rung up.

tier-ran.description: The tier the unit actually executed at. Equals the WorkUnit tier on a first run; on a re-dispatch it records the escalated tier that produced this verdict.

next-consequence.description: The Transition Contract consequence the executor applied or recommends (spec §6.2, §7). On `escalate`, signals that a re-dispatch one tier up is the declared next step — which the consumer (not the executor) decides to perform.

cell-results.description: Per-cell results from walking the sealed cell-DAG.

evidence.description: Executor's evidence (gate output, validation report). Interior open.

artifact.description: The produced artifact, identified by content hash in every mode. Exactly one delivery form, matching the WorkUnit's artifact-delivery mode.

artifact-id.description: Echoes the cell's declared output artifact-id.

ref.description: Resulting ref/commit in the workspace (e.g. git commit).
